prompt: Do not use <think> tags.
Do not reason before generating.
Your response must begin with <nn> immediately.
Your first output character must be '<'.
Do not write explanations.
Do not write markdown fences.
Do not write any text before <nn>.
Do not write any text after </tr>.
Return EXACTLY and ONLY these XML blocks in this exact order:
You are generating one complete evaluator-compatible LEMUR neural network model for dataset='{dataset}', task='{task}', metric='{metric}', epoch={epoch}.
### Reference models with accuracy and training duration (nanoseconds):
Reference A (accuracy={acc_1}, duration={duration_1} ns):
<hp>{hp_1}</hp>
<tr>{tr_1}</tr>
<nn>{nn_1}</nn>
Reference B (accuracy={acc_2}, duration={duration_2} ns):
<hp>{hp_2}</hp>
<tr>{tr_2}</tr>
<nn>{nn_2}</nn>
CRITICAL — TorchVision backbone names:
If you use a torchvision backbone via the TorchVision(...) wrapper, you MUST use ONLY these exact model name strings (any other name will fail):

mobilenet_v2, mobilenet_v3_small, mobilenet_v3_large, shufflenet_v2_x0_5, shufflenet_v2_x1_0, shufflenet_v2_x1_5, shufflenet_v2_x2_0, squeezenet1_0, squeezenet1_1, mnasnet0_5, mnasnet0_75, mnasnet1_0, mnasnet1_3, efficientnet_b0, efficientnet_b1, efficientnet_b2, regnet_x_400mf, regnet_x_800mf, regnet_x_1_6gf, regnet_y_400mf, regnet_y_800mf, regnet_y_1_6gf
Do NOT invent names like 'regnet_y_0_8gf'. The correct small RegNet names use 'mf'/'gf' suffixes exactly as listed above.
### Your task:
Generate a NEW model with HIGHER inference efficiency (accuracy / duration) than BOTH references.
Accuracy may drop at most 1 percentage point below the best reference; computational cost must clearly decrease.
EDGE DEPLOYMENT CONSTRAINTS (mandatory):
- Total trainable parameters: under 2 million.
- Prefer depthwise separable convolutions, inverted residuals, or squeeze-and-excitation blocks.
- Reduce FLOPs compared to both reference models.
The final <nn> code must be a complete runnable LEMUR evaluator-compatible file.
It must contain imports, supported_hyperparameters(), class Net(nn.Module), train_setup(device), and learn(data, target, device).
The <hp> block must contain valid JSON.
The 'transform' value in <hp> must be exactly 'norm_256_flip'.
supported_hyperparameters() must return exactly these keys: 'batch', 'dropout', 'epoch', 'lr', 'momentum', 'transform'.
The <tr> block must contain valid Python code.
